doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document contains fundamentally incorrect legal guidance (Illinois tenants absolutely have the right to cure and cannot be forced to vacate without a court order), uses a fictitious document identifier and future effective date, and mis
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 97
REASON: This document is dangerously inaccurate — under Illinois law a 5-day pay-or-quit notice gives tenants the right to cure by paying rent, a landlord must file an eviction lawsuit and obtain a court order before a tenant is legally required to vacate, and HUD does not co-issue state-specific fact sheets with state attorneys general in this manner; the document number, future effective date (2026), and fabricated legal guidance all indicate this is not a genuine HUD publication.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 97
REASON: The document contains fundamentally incorrect legal guidance (Illinois tenants absolutely have the right to cure and cannot be forced to vacate without a court order), uses a fictitious document identifier and future effective date, and misrepresents a nonexistent HUD/Illinois AG co-issuance process.
